observations.what_customers_say: Some customers use full-service language positively when coordination is concrete and delivered.
observations.decision_relevance: Convenience can be a decision driver if it is backed by specific responsibilities.

observations.what_customers_say: The positive experience extends beyond panel installation to commissioning and registration work.
observations.decision_relevance: Customers may need clarity on the parts of the process that happen after physical installation.

observations.what_customers_say: Carefree-package language works only when the specific included responsibilities are clear.
observations.decision_relevance: Vague all-in language can create expectations that are hard to meet.

observations.what_customers_say: The source context is residential PV where customers connect the project to using solar power at home.
observations.decision_relevance: Self-consumption value should be explained in household terms.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers can value not having to coordinate multiple parties themselves.
observations.decision_relevance: Reducing coordination burden can be a stronger message than adding more technical detail.

cross_source_notes: Full-service language is positive here but risky when unsupported; consumer warning sources caution against vague broad promises.
Registration and commissioning themes overlap with Sunvitec and damm-solar.
caveats: This is a positive review page slice.
Broad full-service claims should be replaced with concrete included tasks.
